evals.prompt: Use agentic mode to implement user profile image uploads end to end. Allow at most two iterations.
evals.expected_output: Receives the natural-language goal, decides Full or Resume mode, and creates a fixed spec path through ywc-plan. For Medium or Large work, runs ywc-spec-validate and ywc-task-generator, then chooses the sequential or parallel executor. Records the pre-iteration SHA before each iteration. The Evaluate phase runs ywc-impl-review against the original spec and the pre-iteration SHA..HEAD git range. If HIGH or CRITICAL findings remain, it does not raise max-iterations; it either re-plans or emits a ceiling report. Appends an iteration record to tasks/agentic-log.md and exits with Completion Status.

evals.prompt: Use $ywc-agentic --resume --tasks-dir tasks/ to continue the existing task run.
evals.expected_output: Detects Resume mode first. If tasks/ is empty or has no pending tasks, it does not silently restart in Full mode; it reports the mismatch and asks for user confirmation. If pending tasks exist, it skips the Plan phase and resumes from the Executor phase using the existing tasks/ directory and dependency graph. It does not re-run tasks already under completed/.

evals.prompt: Only update the copy on a small settings screen. Use agentic and handle it automatically.
evals.expected_output: When ywc-plan returns a Small verdict, uses the Small Path. Does not invoke ywc-task-generator, ywc-sequential-executor, or ywc-parallel-executor; instead runs ywc-code-gen directly. Still preserves pre-iteration SHA recording, ywc-impl-review evaluation, loop control, agentic-log, and Completion Report rules.

evals.prompt: Run agentic mode for a medium feature. The implementation review returns DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with one correctness concern and one observation.
evals.expected_output: Treats the correctness concern as blocking the iteration handoff and routes it back into the implementation loop before marking the iteration complete. Carries only observation-level concerns into the final report. Does not proceed to the next iteration or claim DONE until the correctness concern is fixed and re-reviewed.
